perturbation.original_text: Capitalized terms shall have the meaning ascribed to them in Article 18 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Capitalized terms shall have the meaning ascribed to them throughout this Agreement, unless explicitly redefined within a specific section.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by stating that the defined terms in Article 18 can be overwritten in other sections, leading to potential contradictions throughout the document. For example, the term 'Customer' in Article 18.M has been specifically defined, but this new clause creates ambiguity when cross-referencing 'Customer' throughout the Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: M. "Customer" shall mean the Entity entering into this Agreement with Metavante and all Affiliates of such Entity for whom Metavante agrees to provide Services under this Agreement, as reflected on the first page of this Agreement or amendments executed after the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by allowing the definition of 'Customer,' which is explicitly defined in section M, to be redefined elsewhere in the agreement. This undermines the clarity and enforceability of the original definition, as per the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Metavante warrants that it will provide all Services in a commercially reasonable manner in material conformance with the applicable Documentation (the "Performance Warranty"). Where the parties have agreed upon Service Levels for any aspect of Metavante's performance, such Service Levels shall apply in lieu of the Performance Warranty.
perturbation.changed_text: Metavante warrants that it will use its best efforts to provide all Services in a manner that aligns with industry standards and practices as understood in good faith, referencing the applicable Documentation as a guide (the "Performance Warranty"). Where the parties have agreed upon Service Levels for any aspect of Metavante's performance, those Service Levels are aspirational targets, and do not replace the Performance Warranty but provide guidance on performance expectations.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens Metavante’s guarantee. Originally, Metavante warranted to provide Services in 'material conformance' with the documentation and agreed service levels would directly apply instead of this warranty. The new text lowers the standard to 'best efforts' and states Service Levels don't replace the Performance Warranty, which creates an **internal contradiction** because Service Levels define specific performance metrics; this contradiction undermines the enforceability of the explicit Service Levels defined later in the Service Level Schedule because the targets are now 'aspirational'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Service Levels, if any, relating to a particular Service shall be as set forth in the Service Level Schedule. The parties agree that Metavante's performance of Services at a level at or above any Service Level shall be satisfactory performance. Metavante shall cure any failure to achieve a Service Level within the period specified within the applicable schedule. Remedies, if any, for failure to achieve a Service Level shall be as set forth in the Service Level Schedule.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Service Levels apply 'in lieu of the Performance Warranty,' implying they replace it. The changed text says Service Levels 'do not replace the Performance Warranty' and are only 'aspirational targets.' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that achieving Service Levels constitutes 'satisfactory performance' and mandates specific remedies for failure to meet them. This inconsistency creates ambiguity and makes the enforceability of the Service Level Schedule questionable, representing a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: A. Customer shall be solely responsible for monitoring and interpreting (and for complying with, to the extent such compliance requires no action by Metavante) the Legal Requirements. Based on Customer's instructions, Metavante shall select the processing parameter settings, features, and options (collectively, the "Parameters") within Metavante's system that will apply to Customer. Customer shall be responsible for determining that such selections are consistent with the Legal Requirements and with the terms and conditions of any agreements between Customer and its clients. In making such determinations, Customer may rely upon the written descriptions of such Parameters contained in the User Manuals.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Metavante will offer suggestions regarding Legal Requirements, and the Customer retains final responsibility for decisions regarding Legal Requirements, to the extent such compliance requires no action by Metavante. Based on Customer's instructions and Metavante's advice, Metavante shall select the processing parameter settings, features, and options (collectively, the "Parameters") within Metavante's system that will apply to Customer. Metavante shall make reasonable efforts to assist Customer in determining that such selections are consistent with the Legal Requirements, while Customer remains responsible for ultimate interpretation. In making such determinations, Customer may rely upon the written descriptions of such Parameters contained in the User Manuals, but Metavante does not guarantee any particular outcome.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces shared responsibility for ensuring compliance with legal requirements, where the original placed it solely on the Customer. It makes the original section **contradictory** because Metavante now has an advisory role and assists with making determinations, undermining the Customer's sole responsibility for those decisions. It is also stated that 'Metavante does not guarantee any particular outcome.' Also the clause 'while Customer remains responsible for ultimate interpretation' is contradicting the help that Metavante is providing, where they will 'select the processing parameter settings, features, and options'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Customer shall be solely responsible for monitoring and interpreting (and for complying with, to the extent such compliance requires no action by Metavante) the Legal Requirements. Based on Customer's instructions, Metavante shall select the processing parameter settings, features, and options (collectively, the "Parameters") within Metavante's system that will apply to Customer. Customer shall be responsible for determining that such selections are consistent with the Legal Requirements and with the terms and conditions of any agreements between Customer and its clients. In making such determinations, Customer may rely upon the written descriptions of such Parameters contained in the User Manuals.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the 'Customer shall be solely responsible', while the changed text introduces shared responsibility with Metavante. Metavante now offers suggestions and assistance ('Metavante will offer suggestions regarding Legal Requirements...Metavante shall make reasonable efforts to assist Customer'). This directly contradicts the initial statement of sole responsibility, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' where key obligations are defined inconsistently, making interpretation unclear. The addition of Metavante's advisory role and assistance undermines the Customer's previously defined sole responsibility, creating a significant and obvious contradiction.
